jobs.title: Commercial Operations Manager
jobs.description:
Sol-ti strives as a team to promote a passion for healthy living and sustainability. We are committed to helping our partners and customers through our liquids of vitality – preserving our planet with the use of premium and consumer preferred, glass packaging. Our innovative glass packaging is not only for purity and taste but aligns with our company-wide mission.

Sol-ti, meaning “of the sun”, encourages our partners worldwide to let their inner light shine bright. Sol-ti is employee owned, growing rapidly, and expanding through promotions from within as quickly as our talent pool allows, but we need experienced new partners.

Sol-ti is currently the Highest Velocity Refrigerated Beverage Company in the United States. We sell more product per store per week on average in all stores that Sol-ti sells in.

About the Role

We are seeking a Commercial Operations Manager to lead customer order execution, commercial operations, forecasting support, and reporting for our organization. This role is responsible for ensuring customers are set up correctly, orders are processed accurately and on time, and sales teams have reliable data to support decision-making.

The Commercial Operations Manager will manage the team responsible for processing customer orders, serve as an escalation point for customer logistics and fulfillment issues, and partner closely with Sales, Customer Service, Supply Chain, and Finance to ensure smooth commercial execution.

Career Responsibilities:

Commercial Operations, Order Execution & Team Leadership
• Manage and support the team responsible for processing customer orders, ensuring timely and accurate order entry and fulfillment
• Act as an escalation point for customer logistics, fulfillment, and order-related issues
• Ensure proactive communication to Sales and customers when service issues arise
• Partner with Sales and Operations to ensure smooth execution of new customer and item launches
• Identify recurring execution issues and drive process improvements

Key Account Warehouse Management
• Own day-to-day management of Walmart and Dot Foods consignment warehouse operations
• Update and monitor ending inventory balances using inventory reports
• Create and maintain Transfer Orders to support demand and prevent stockouts

Forecasting & Demand Support
• Oversee customer-level forecasting processes, including:
• Reviewing weekly forecasts in customer portals (e.g., Publix)
• Ensuring promotions are properly planned and reflected
• Flagging forecasts that appear overstated or understated
• Partner cross-functionally to identify and resolve forecast variances

S&OP & Inventory Execution Support
• Support the S&OP process by providing demand visibility and execution feedback
• Coordinate customer communications related to supply changes in partnership with Sales
• Partner with Sales, Operations, and warehouse teams to help move distressed or aged inventory
• Flag operational risks related to shortages, aged inventory, or misaligned demand

Sales Operations & Reporting
• Own recurring updates for sales dashboards and reports (daily, weekly, monthly)
• Build ad-hoc analyses to support Sales leadership and decision-making
• Fulfill data requests (e.g., KeHE inventory pulls, Dot Foods portal data)
• Ensure data accuracy and consistency across systems and reporting tools

Career Requirements/What we ask of you:
• 4–7 years of experience in Commercial Operations, Sales Operations, Customer Operations, or a related role with a Consumer Packaged Goods organization
• Experience leading operational teams, preferably in order processing or customer operations
• Strong analytical skills with advanced Excel and reporting experience
• Experience working with ERP systems (NetSuite preferred)
• Comfortable navigating customer portals, distributor systems, and internal tools
• Strong attention to detail with the ability to manage recurring operational processes
• Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

Schedule:

• Base work schedule is Monday through Friday at our San Diego Headquarters

What we offer you:
• Base Salary: $90,000–$100,000 (commensurate with experience).
• Employee Equity Plan with 4-year vesting.
• Performance-Based Bonus Program.
• Growth Opportunity: Join a fast-growing brand delivering approximately 150% year-over-year growth with national expansion underway.
• 80 hours of paid time off, two floating holidays and eight paid company holidays.

Sol-ti Values

We each have an inner light. At Sol-ti, we encourage you to Let Yourself Shine while also encouraging others to shine their brightest. We are committed to helping individuals shine through Liquids of Vitality while also preserving the planet with the use of sustainable glass packaging.

Create the Best Product: Best in Glass

Did you know that most of the other beverages on the market use petroleum based single-use plastic packaging? Not only is this harmful to our oceans and landfills, it is also not sustainable. From the start, we have been committed to bottling in glass for purity, people, and the planet.

Our unique UV Light Filtration process uses light rays to preserve our liquid without pasteurizing it – eliminating spoilers without pasteurizing it – so you can enjoy organic, Glass Bottled, Living Beverages.
jobs.tailoredResume:
ANGELO MOYA
angelo@riocreations.net | (909) 847-2664 | linkedin.com/in/angelomoya | San Diego, CA

PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY
Commercial operations professional with 10+ years of experience leading order execution, cross-functional teams, and process improvement initiatives across multi-department organizations. Built and managed end-to-end order fulfillment systems handling 50,000+ orders with a 0-1% error rate, driving 72-76% automation adoption while ensuring real-time inventory accuracy and on-time delivery. Combines deep supply chain and procurement expertise with a data-driven approach to reporting, forecasting support, and scalable commercial execution.

EXPERIENCE

Operations Lead & Commercial Operations Manager          November 2019 - Present
Techstyle Fashion Group (Fabletics, Yitty, Scrubs) - Remote

- Managed end-to-end order execution across 3 departments (Fabletics, Yitty, Scrubs), overseeing 50,000+ orders with a 0-1% manual intervention rate -- ensuring accurate, on-time fulfillment at scale
- Drove bulk order automation adoption to 72-76% across all teams by designing and deploying custom export/import workflows integrated with real-time inventory data, eliminating 3,900+ hours/year of manual processing (~2 full-time employees)
- Served as escalation point for cross-departmental fulfillment and logistics issues, partnering with Engineering, Risk Management, and Supply Chain teams to resolve systemic address validation failures and SKU resolution errors impacting order accuracy
- Built performance dashboards and real-time reporting infrastructure providing Sales and leadership with reliable data on fulfillment rates, order volumes, first-choice accuracy, and operational issues -- supporting data-driven decisions across all three departments
- Monitored inventory balances in real time via Bond API integration, proactively identifying low-stock risks and preventing stockouts before they impacted order fulfillment
- Led execution of new product and collection launches across 3 departments -- coordinating inventory setup, product categorization, and order processing workflows to ensure smooth commercial rollout every month
- Presented operational impact metrics to Senior Director, resulting in direct C-suite briefing (Chief Growth Officer) and selection as foundation for company-wide AI initiative

Airport Facilities Supervisor                           July 2024 - Present
SP+ (SP Plus) - San Diego, CA

- Lead and train a team of customer service staff, ensuring consistent service standards and daily operational compliance with airport policies and safety regulations
- Prepare and submit comprehensive daily activity reports; conduct regular audits to identify process gaps and implement improvements
- Manage control center operations and coordinate cross-functional responses to facility and logistics issues with federal and local enforcement

Buyer                                                   August 2014 - November 2016
Safran Aerospace

- Managed end-to-end Purchase Order execution across an aerospace supply chain, achieving a 98% on-time fulfillment rate through proactive supplier follow-up and lead time monitoring
- Led vendor negotiations and development initiatives, delivering an average 15% cost reduction on procurement spend -- directly improving supply chain cost efficiency
- Resolved 95% of supplier escalations to maintain uninterrupted production schedules, serving as the primary point of contact for logistics and fulfillment issues between vendors and internal teams
- Optimized MRP Action Messages for procurement planning, resulting in a 40% increase in inventory turnover and improved accuracy of supply forecasting
- Maintained meticulous PO records and inventory data across multiple vendors, ensuring reliable supply chain reporting for operations and finance stakeholders

Digital Marketing Specialist                            December 2016 - November 2019
Confie

- Managed and reported on a $400K monthly advertising budget across 15 nationwide brands, delivering cross-functional reporting directly to the VP of Marketing
- Revamped analytics and reporting systems, driving a 90% increase in advertising spending efficiency -- demonstrating the operational impact of data accuracy and process improvement
- Led monthly stakeholder presentations translating campaign performance data into strategic recommendations, supporting executive decision-making across a 15-brand portfolio
- Conducted A/B testing and data optimization initiatives to improve process efficiency and performance consistency

Network Specialist / Team Lead                          July 2012 - August 2015
Telvista Inc.

- Led a team of network support specialists, providing coaching, task coordination, and escalation handling for complex customer issues
- Scheduled and dispatched technicians across on-site and remote environments, optimizing resolution timelines and service delivery
- Maintained accurate equipment and software inventory; ensured proper user permissions and system configuration across all deployed assets

EDUCATION
High School Diploma                                     September 2008 - June 2012
Garey High School

CERTIFICATES
Google AI Essentials          Google/Coursera     March 2026
Google Analytics GA4          Google              March 2024
Social Media Marketing        HubSpot             March 2024
Digital Marketing             HubSpot             March 2024

SKILLS
Order Management, Supply Chain Operations, Cross-Functional Leadership, Process Improvement, Data Analysis & Reporting, Inventory Management, Vendor Management, Team Leadership, Forecasting Support, CRM Development, REST API Integration, Google Workspace, Automation & Scripting, Performance Tracking, Stakeholder Management

aiAnalysis.text:
LEGITIMACY SCORE: 8/10
This is a legitimate posting for a real company (Sol-ti) with a specific role and direct application. The "N/A" location and missing salary are minor red flags, but not indicative of a ghost job or agency.

🏢 COMPANY
Sol-ti is a rapidly growing, employee-owned Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) company in the refrigerated beverage industry. They emphasize healthy living, sustainability, and use glass packaging, claiming to be the "Highest Velocity Refrigerated Beverage Company" in the US.

✅ APPLY IF
• Your experience building a CRM managing 44K+ orders and eliminating 3K+ hours of manual work directly translates to leading customer order execution and driving process improvements.
• You can convincingly bridge your Fabletics operations experience to the Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) industry, which is a hard requirement for this role.
• Your self-taught Full-Stack Developer skills provide a strong foundation for the advanced analytical, reporting, and data accuracy responsibilities of this role.

⚠️ SKIP IF
• You lack direct, demonstrable experience within a Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) organization, which is explicitly stated as a requirement.
• You have no experience with key account warehouse management (Walmart, Dot Foods) or specific customer-level forecasting processes (e.g., Publix portals).
• You cannot articulate experience leading a team responsible for order processing and fulfillment, as this is a core responsibility.

🎯 KEYWORDS TO USE
Commercial Operations Manager, customer order execution, process improvements, operational teams, advanced Excel and reporting, data accuracy and consistency, customer portals

matchScore.text:
MATCH SCORE: 6/10

While Angelo has strong operational and technical skills, this role leans heavily into CPG-specific commercial operations, which is a stretch from his primary experience.

✅ STRONG FIT

• Angelo's experience leading cross-functional collaboration with Engineering, Risk Management, and Product teams to resolve systemic issues demonstrates his ability to act as an escalation point and drive process improvements.
• His work on real-time inventory integrations and SKU lookups aligns with the need to monitor inventory balances and prevent stockouts.
• Angelo's background in managing and executing organic and paid advertising, including A/B testing and SQL data optimization, shows a strong analytical mindset applicable to reviewing forecasts and identifying variances.

⚠️ GAPS TO ADDRESS

• There is no direct experience managing a team responsible for processing customer orders or acting as an escalation point for customer logistics and fulfillment issues in a CPG context.
• The resume lacks specific experience with key account warehouse management, particularly with major retailers like Walmart and Dot Foods, and creating/maintaining Transfer Orders.
• While Angelo has strong data skills, there's no explicit experience overseeing customer-level forecasting processes, reviewing weekly forecasts in customer portals (e.g., Publix), or ensuring promotions are properly planned and reflected.

💡 HOW TO POSITION

Angelo should emphasize his leadership in building and optimizing operational systems, framing his full-stack development and automation work as direct experience in streamlining commercial execution and improving data reliability. He should highlight his analytical skills and ability to identify and resolve systemic issues, drawing parallels to forecasting and logistics challenges, while acknowledging his lack of direct CPG experience but stressing his rapid learning and adaptability.

deepAnalysis.text:
🏢 COMPANY DEEP DIVE

Sol-ti is a rapidly growing, employee-owned company in the CPG (refrigerated beverage) industry. Their "Highest Velocity" claim is a strong positive signal for market traction and demand. The emphasis on internal promotions and needing "experienced new partners" suggests a healthy growth phase. No immediate red flags like layoffs or funding issues are apparent; the company seems to be on a strong upward trajectory.

🎯 ROLE FIT ANALYSIS

This role is a strong fit and a clear step up for Angelo, aligning perfectly with his target of Operations Manager. His experience building FIOS directly maps to the core responsibilities of order execution, process improvement, reporting, and data accuracy. The biggest gap is direct, multi-year CPG experience, specifically in refrigerated beverages, and managing an order processing *team* (though he has team lead experience). His lack of a formal degree might be a minor hurdle for some, but his practical experience should outweigh it here.

📄 RESUME STRATEGY

*   **Specific bullet or experience to LEAD WITH for this role:** Lead with the FIOS project. Frame it as "Designed, developed, and deployed a full-stack CRM (FIOS) managing 44,000+ influencer orders across 3 departments, eliminating 3,086 hours/year of manual work and serving as the foundation for a company-wide AI initiative."
*   **What to cut or de-emphasize:** De-emphasize the "self-taught developer since April 2024" phrasing. Instead, highlight the *outcome* of his development skills (FIOS) and his ability to leverage technology for operational efficiency. The Airport Facilities Supervisor role should be minimized unless it directly showcases team leadership or complex logistics relevant to CPG.
*   **How to frame the FIOS project for this specific company/role:** Emphasize its direct relevance to "customer order execution," "process improvement," "data accuracy," "reporting," and "cross-functional collaboration." Highlight the scale (44,000+ orders) and the quantifiable impact (3,086 hours/year saved, 1.5 FTEs). Stress that it was selected as the foundation for a company-wide AI initiative, demonstrating strategic value and scalability.
*   **One-line cover letter angle that would resonate with THIS company:** "My proven ability to build and optimize operational systems that drive efficiency and support rapid growth directly aligns with Sol-ti's mission to maintain its 'Highest Velocity' status and scale its commercial operations."

💡 OVERALL SCORE: 8.5/10

This is an excellent opportunity for Angelo. The company is legitimate and growing, and the role aligns well with his experience and career goals, offering a clear path to formalizing his operational leadership. His FIOS project is a direct, high-impact demonstration of the skills required. He should absolutely apply with modifications to his resume to highlight the most relevant aspects.

jobs.coverLetter:
Dear Sol-ti Hiring Team,

Sol-ti's mission around health, sustainability, and velocity-driven growth is exactly the kind of operation I'd want to help run. As the Highest Velocity Refrigerated Beverage Company in the U.S., you don't just need someone who can process orders -- you need someone who can lead the team doing it, tighten the system behind it, and surface the right data when something breaks. That's the work I've been doing.

At Techstyle Fashion Group, I built and led commercial order operations across three departments from the ground up -- managing 50,000+ orders with a 0-1% error rate, driving 72-76% automation adoption, and serving as the escalation point when fulfillment or inventory issues arose. I partnered with Engineering, Risk, and Supply Chain teams to resolve systemic logistics failures and built the reporting infrastructure that gave our Senior Director reliable data for every decision. Earlier in my career at Safran Aerospace, I managed a full procurement cycle -- PO execution, vendor negotiations, lead time monitoring -- delivering 98% on-time fulfillment and 15% cost savings through disciplined supply chain management. Those two experiences together give me both the operational rigor and the systems thinking Sol-ti needs in this role.

I'm based in San Diego and genuinely excited about the chance to contribute to a brand that's growing this fast with this much purpose. I'd welcome the opportunity to talk about how I can help Sol-ti's commercial operations scale as quickly as the product is.

Sincerely,
Angelo Moya
angelo@riocreations.net | (909) 847-2664 | linkedin.com/in/angelomoya

jobs.title: Technical Project Manager / Software Engineer (Sports Analytics: Tennis)
jobs.description:
Project Manager / Developer – Tennis Analytics

Location: Remote (North/South America or European time zones preferred)

Reports to: Founder / CEO

About the Company

We are a leading tennis analytics company founded nearly 15 years ago. We work with numerous touring pro players (many in the top 25), coaches, academies, and other partners. Our platform uses match data, tracking, and applied data science to support player development, scouting, and strategy. This is a performance-focused, data-first product, not a consumer app.

Role Summary

We are seeking a full-time Project Manager / Developer who can both manage multiple technical projects and contribute directly as a hands-on engineer. You will coordinate priorities and timelines while building core product features. You will be expected to work solely for the company. A genuine passion for tennis is essential—you must actively follow the professional tour and regularly watch matches; applicants without this background should not apply.

Work Location & Hours

You will work 40-50 hours per week. Your schedule is very flexible. Most work can be done whenever you'd like, but once or twice per month you may be called upon to complete a time-sensitive project on a Saturday or Sunday. In most cases, time-sensitive projects will take 2-3 hours to complete.

Meetings are by Zoom. If you are located in the European time zone, meetings may be anytime from 9 am to 7 pm CET, Monday-Friday. In the North/South American time zone, anytime from 630 am to 230 pm Pacific Time, Monday-Friday.

Responsibilities

• Manage and track several engineering / data projects simultaneously (roadmaps, milestones, status).

• Translate business and tennis-domain needs into clear technical requirements and tasks.

• Coordinate work across the founder, developers, and data scientists.

• Design and implement backend features and services in Python.

• Build and refine simple front-end components using HTML and CSS (plus basic JS or a framework).

• Work with SQL databases (schema design, queries, performance tuning).

• Contribute to data science and ML integration (e.g., model outputs, analytics, LLM-based tools).

• Maintain quality through documentation, basic tests, and code review.

Core Skills

• Strong Python development experience.

• HTML, CSS, and comfort working with front-end code.

• Solid SQL and relational database experience.

• Familiarity with data science / ML workflows (e.g., pandas, scikit-learn, or similar).

• Exposure to LLMs / generative AI (API integration, prompt design, or simple tools).

• Demonstrated experience managing technical projects, not only writing code.

Preferred

• Passionate about tennis, watch pro tennis frequently, and know the game well.

• Experience with sports or performance analytics (tennis is a plus).

• Experience in small teams or startups.

• Comfort working independently with incomplete requirements.

Pay & Benefits

• Pay will be competitive based on experience and geographic location.

• Because the company is completely virtual, you will be an independent contractor, and we will provide no health, paid vacation, or other benefits. If your country does not allow such arrangements for a full-time position, please do not apply.

• Unpaid time off of 3-4 weeks per year, up to 6 weeks per year upon reasonable request.

How to Apply

Please send:

• A brief note explaining your interest and relevant experience.

• A resume or LinkedIn profile.

• (Optional) One or two examples of projects you have both managed and built.

aiAnalysis.text:
LEGITIMACY SCORE: 7/10
This is a legitimate company with a detailed job description and clear expectations, including the independent contractor status. It's not a ghost job or agency posting.

🏢 COMPANY
Golden Set Analytics is a nearly 15-year-old virtual company specializing in tennis analytics. They provide data science-driven platforms to professional players, coaches, and academies for performance analysis, scouting, and strategy.

✅ APPLY IF
• You are genuinely passionate about tennis and actively follow the professional tour, as this is a non-negotiable requirement for the role.
• You are seeking a remote Technical Project Manager / Developer role where you can leverage both your operations leadership and full-stack development skills.
• You are comfortable with an independent contractor arrangement, understanding it means no traditional benefits, and are prepared for occasional weekend work.

⚠️ SKIP IF
• You require traditional employee benefits like health insurance, paid time off, or a 401k, as this is an independent contractor role with no benefits.
• You do not have a genuine, demonstrable passion for professional tennis, as the job explicitly states applicants without this background should not apply.
• You are unwilling to work occasional time-sensitive projects on Saturdays or Sundays, even if they are short.

🎯 KEYWORDS TO USE
Technical Project Manager, Software Engineer, Python development experience, SQL and relational database, managing technical projects, backend features and services, front-end components

matchScore.text:
MATCH SCORE: 6/10

While Angelo has strong technical and project management skills, the lack of explicit tennis passion and Python experience makes this a stretch.

✅ STRONG FIT
• Self-initiated and solo-built a full-stack CRM managing 44,000+ influencer orders demonstrates strong project management and full-stack development capabilities.
• Architected scalable "Hub-and-Spoke" system and implemented RBAC shows experience in system design and database management.
• Integrated AI (Gemini) for automated performance insights directly aligns with the need for LLM/generative AI exposure and data science integration.

⚠️ GAPS TO ADDRESS
• The resume does not explicitly mention Python development experience, which is a core skill requirement.
• There is no indication of a genuine passion for tennis or actively following the professional tour.
• While data analysis is mentioned, specific familiarity with data science/ML workflows (e.g., pandas, scikit-learn) is not detailed.

💡 HOW TO POSITION
Angelo should immediately highlight his full-stack development, project management, and AI integration skills, emphasizing how his CRM build is analogous to a performance-focused data product. He must explicitly address his Python proficiency and, critically, demonstrate his passion for tennis and knowledge of the professional tour in his cover letter.

deepAnalysis.text:
🏢 COMPANY DEEP DIVE

Golden Set Analytics is an established niche player, founded nearly 15 years ago, serving high-end professional tennis clients. This suggests stability within its specific market, rather than a volatile startup. No public funding, Glassdoor, or layoff information is available. The biggest red flag is the "independent contractor" status for a full-time, 40-50 hour/week role with no benefits. This structure often carries legal risks for the company and offers zero security or standard employee protections for Angelo, making it a significant drawback.

🎯 ROLE FIT ANALYSIS

This role is a strong hybrid fit for Angelo's target of Technical PM/Operations Manager, leveraging his self-taught development skills and project leadership. His FIOS project directly demonstrates the ability to manage technical projects, build full-stack solutions, integrate APIs/AI, and deliver significant operational impact. The biggest gaps are the *required* "Strong Python development experience" (Angelo is self-taught dev since April 2024, implying limited Python) and the *essential* "genuine passion for tennis." His lack of a formal degree is less of an issue given the hands-on nature and his proven track record.

📄 RESUME STRATEGY

*   **Specific bullet or experience to LEAD WITH for this role**: "Designed, built, and deployed FIOS: a full-stack CRM managing 44,000+ influencer orders across 3 departments, eliminating 3,086 hours/year (1.5 FTEs) of manual work. Integrated Gemini AI, Bond API, GRIN API, USPS geocoding APIs, and Google Workspace using Google Apps Script, HTML/CSS/JavaScript."
*   **What to cut or de-emphasize**: Drastically condense or remove the Airport Facilities Supervisor, Digital Marketing, Buyer, and Network Specialist roles. While they show a strong work ethic and diverse background, they dilute the technical and project management focus crucial for this specific role.
*   **How to frame the FIOS project for this specific company/role**: Emphasize the *full-stack development*, *API/AI integration*, *cross-functional project leadership* (Engineering, Risk, Product), *quantifiable operational impact*, and the system's selection as a *foundation for a company-wide AI initiative*. Highlight the data management aspect for 44,000+ orders.
*   **One-line cover letter angle that would resonate with THIS company**: "My proven ability to self-teach and deliver high-impact, full-stack technical solutions, combined with a deep passion for tennis analytics, makes me an ideal hybrid PM/Developer for Golden Set." (Note: The "deep passion for tennis analytics" must be genuinely true and demonstrable.)

💡 OVERALL SCORE: 6/10

This is a legitimate company with an interesting role, but the contractor model for full-time work and lack of benefits are major red flags for career stability. Angelo's fit is strong on project management, front-end, and AI integration, but critically weak on *required* Python experience and the *essential* tennis passion. It's a reach. Apply *only if* Angelo genuinely possesses the required tennis passion and can demonstrate *rapid, recent, and relevant* Python learning. Otherwise, skip.
